amount_description: 100% waiver of tuition, admission, examination, laboratory, library, and hostel fees in government institutions. Reimbursement up to ₹5,00,000 for non-government unaided institutions.
benefits: Complete fee waiver for all educational expenses including tuition fees admission and readmission fees examination fees hostel admission fees laboratory fees library fees college development fees and all other institutional charges, Free accommodation and food provided in government institutions where available, Reimbursement up to ₹5,00,000 for complete course duration in non-government unaided private institutions, In government and aided colleges fees exempted at admission with government reimbursing institution directly, In unaided private institutions students pay upfront and claim reimbursement later with maximum cap ₹5 lakh for entire course, Covers COVID-19 orphans who lost single or both parents during pandemic, No restriction on course type - all general professional technical courses eligible, Support continues for entire course duration till completion
docs_needed: Aadhaar card, Death certificate of parent or parents, Residential certificate proving Odisha domicile, Income certificate from Tahasildar showing guardian or surviving parent income below ₹8 lakh, Bank passbook copy with account number and IFSC, Enrollment proof or admission letter from institution, Previous educational qualification certificates, Recent passport size photograph, Specially Cared Child Certificate or I-Card if COVID-19 orphan, DCPO certification if child was in Child Care Institution
step_guide: 1. Download application form from https://dhe.odisha.gov.in/sites/default/files/2021-06/GREEN PASSAGE-4-10.pdf, 2. Fill application form completely with all required details, 3. Gather all required documents including death certificate income certificate and enrollment proof, 4. If child was in Child Care Institution obtain certification from DCPO District Child Protection Officer, 5. If COVID-19 orphan obtain Specially Cared Child Certificate or I-Card, 6. Submit completed application with documents to DCPO or Child Welfare Committee offline, 7. DCPO verifies documents and forwards to Higher Education Department, 8. Department prepares merit list based on academic performance and family income, 9. Results announced publicly by department, 10. For government and aided colleges fees exempted at admission, 11. For unaided private colleges students pay fees and apply for reimbursement with fee receipts and vouchers, 12. Reimbursement transferred to student Aadhaar-linked bank account via DBT
selection: Based on validation of orphan status (loss of both parents or single bread-earning parent) by the DCPO/CWC, and parental/guardian income ≤ ₹8.0 Lakh.
renewal: Satisfactory academic performance and attendance, verified annually by the Principal of the college.
intro_seo: The Green Passage Scheme Higher Education provides complete fee waiver and reimbursement up to ₹5 lakh for orphaned and vulnerable students in Odisha pursuing any higher education course after Class 12 with full exemption of tuition hostel admission examination laboratory library fees plus free accommodation and food in government institutions.
faq_json: [{"question":"Who is eligible for the Green Passage Scheme in Odisha?","answer":"Orphan students who have lost both parents, single parent, or whose biological/adoptive parents are deceased, and who are pursuing higher education in recognized institutions in Odisha are eligible."},{"question":"What educational benefits does the Green Passage Scheme provide?","answer":"The scheme provides a complete waiver of application, admission, tuition, examination, laboratory, library, and hostel fees in government institutions. For non-government unaided institutions, reimbursement is capped at ₹5,00,000 for the entire course."},{"question":"What is the family income limit for the Green Passage Scheme?","answer":"The annual income of the surviving parent or legal guardian must not exceed ₹8,00,000 (8 Lakh) to qualify for the scheme's benefits."}]

title: Green Passage Scheme - Technical Education (Odisha)
caste: General, SC, ST, OBC, SEBC, EBC
amount_description: Provides a complete fee waiver (admission, tuition, examination, hostel, laboratory, library, etc.) for orphaned students pursuing higher/technical/professional education. For government and government-aided colleges, the waiver is direct. For private colleges, fees are reimbursed up to a maximum cap of ₹5,00,000 for the entire course duration. Payments/reimbursements are processed via Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) to the student's or institution's bank account.
benefits: Variable scholarship amounts based on course and institution type, Fee waiver or reimbursement for admission tuition examination and other institutional charges, Support for orphaned and vulnerable students pursuing technical and vocational education, Covers ITI Industrial Training Institute courses in all trades, Covers ITC Industrial Training Center courses, Covers Polytechnic diploma courses in engineering and technology, Covers other diploma courses in technical fields, Direct Benefit Transfer to student Aadhaar-linked bank account, Scholarship continues for course duration, Application through online Odisha State Scholarship Portal, Merit-based selection considering academic performance and orphan status
docs_needed: Aadhaar card, Death certificate of parent or parents, Residential certificate, Income certificate from Tahasildar showing guardian income below ₹8 lakh, Bank passbook copy with account number and IFSC Aadhaar-linked, Enrollment proof or admission letter from ITI Polytechnic institution, Previous educational qualification certificates Class 10 or equivalent, Recent passport size photograph, College or institution ID card
step_guide: 1. Visit scholarship.odisha.gov.in and click Registration if new user, 2. Read instructions tick declaration checkbox and click Proceed, 3. Enter Aadhaar number and click Get OTP, 4. Validate OTP received on Aadhaar-registered mobile number, 5. Complete registration with basic details and create password, 6. Login with unique number or Aadhaar number and password, 7. Navigate to Schemes section and look for Green Passage for Orphans Students, 8. Click Apply button next to scheme name, 9. Fill application form with personal details orphan status details and academic information, 10. Upload all required documents including death certificate income certificate and enrollment proof, 11. Complete Aadhaar-based verification if required, 12. Submit application and download acknowledgment receipt, 13. Institution principal will verify application online, 14. Track application status through student dashboard on portal
selection: Selected based on orphan status (having lost both biological/adoptive parents or single breadwinner due to COVID-19). Must be domiciled in Odisha and have a guardian family income not exceeding ₹8 Lakhs per annum.
renewal: Requires promotion to the next academic year, maintaining satisfactory academic performance, clean conduct, minimum attendance as per university norms, and verification by the institutional head.
intro_seo: The Green Passage Scheme Technical Education provides financial assistance and fee support to orphaned students in Odisha pursuing ITI ITC Polytechnic and Diploma courses with variable scholarship amounts covering educational expenses for vulnerable children without parental support in vocational and technical education.
faq_json: [{"question":"Who is eligible under the Green Passage Scheme in Odisha?","answer":"Orphan students who have lost both parents or the single earning parent (including due to COVID-19) and are pursuing higher/technical/professional education are eligible."},{"question":"What is the maximum reimbursement for private institutions?","answer":"For non-government unaided (private) institutions, the fee reimbursement is capped at a maximum of ₹5,00,000 for the entire duration of the course."},{"question":"What expenses are covered under this scheme?","answer":"The scheme covers admission, tuition, examination, hostel, laboratory, library, and college development fees, as well as providing free lodging and food."}]

title: Gopabandhu Sikhya Sahayata Yojana (Odisha)
amount_description: ₹20,000 per annum for the normal duration of the course. Paid via Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) directly into the student's Aadhaar-seeded bank account.
benefits: Annual financial assistance of ₹20,000 per student, Assistance provided for entire duration of UG/PG course, Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) to student's Aadhaar-linked bank account, Can be availed along with other scholarships simultaneously (unique feature), Covers both technical and non-technical courses, Available for professional and non-professional programs, No restriction on receiving multiple government scholarships, Supports students from 10 vulnerable categories, Aims to increase Gross Enrollment Ratio in higher education, Reduces dropout rates among marginalized communities
docs_needed: Aadhaar card, Recent passport size photograph, Income certificate from Tahasildar, Caste certificate if applicable, Residence certificate, Bank passbook with Aadhaar-linked account, Last qualifying exam marksheet and certificate, Admission letter or College ID card, Category-specific certificate (HIV/AIDS medical certificate, Homeless certificate from revenue authority, Single mother certificate from DSWO, Destitute certificate from Sarpanch/ULB, Manual scavenger certificate, PVTG certificate from DWO, Bonded labourer release certificate, Orphan certificate from DPO, Transgender certificate from National Portal, Child marriage rescue certificate from DSWO - as applicable)
step_guide: 1. Visit State Scholarship Portal at https://scholarship.odisha.gov.in/, 2. Click on Registration and read Instructions/Declaration carefully, 3. Select checkbox and click I Agree, 4. Enter Aadhaar number and mobile number to proceed, 5. Fill mandatory fields: Name, Gender, Date of Birth, Email ID, 6. Receive User ID and Password on registered mobile/email, 7. Click Student Login and enter credentials, 8. Select Schemes and Scholarship option from menu, 9. Choose Gopabandhu Sikhya Sahayata Yojana from dropdown, 10. Fill complete application form with student details, academic background, family income, bank details, 11. Upload all required documents and category-specific certificates, 12. Review filled application carefully, 13. Submit online application and download acknowledgment, 14. Submit two printed copies with documents to Principal/Head of Institution for verification
selection: Based on annual family income (≤ ₹2,50,000) and belonging to designated vulnerable categories (HIV/AIDS, manual scavengers, transgender, orphans, shelter-less, single mothers, etc.).
renewal: Renewed annually for the course duration, subject to continued enrollment and verification by the Head of the Institution. No backlogs are generally allowed.
intro_seo: The Gopabandhu Sikhya Sahayata Yojana provides ₹20,000 per year to vulnerable and marginalized students in Odisha pursuing undergraduate and postgraduate courses in government-recognized institutions with family income below ₹2.5 lakh annually including HIV/AIDS affected families, homeless households, single mother families, orphans, transgender students, manual scavengers, bonded laborers, PVTGs, and girls rescued from child marriage.
faq_json: [{"question":"What is the Gopabandhu Sikhya Sahayata Yojana and who provides it?","answer":"It is a financial assistance scheme provided by the Higher Education Department, Government of Odisha, offering ₹20,000 per year to underprivileged students pursuing graduate or postgraduate courses."},{"question":"What is the family income limit and who belongs to the target group?","answer":"The annual family income must not exceed ₹2.5 Lakh. The applicant must belong to designated vulnerable categories like HIV/AIDS affected families, manual scavengers, transgender, orphans, single mothers, etc."},{"question":"Is this scholarship applicable to technical and professional courses?","answer":"Yes, students enrolled in regular graduate or postgraduate programs, including technical, professional, and non-professional streams, are eligible to apply."}]

title: Shahid Madho Singh Haath Kharcha Yojana (Odisha)
benefits: One-time financial assistance of ₹5,000 at time of admission into Class 9 or Class 11, Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) to student's Aadhaar-linked bank account after school verification, Aims to reduce dropout rate among ST students after Class 8 and Class 10, Can be availed along with other scholarship schemes including Post-Matric ST scholarship, Targeted towards 3 lakh ST students across Odisha, Named after tribal freedom fighter Shaheed Madho Singh, Scheme launched on martyrdom day (December 31, 2024) of Shaheed Madho Singh, Covers both boys and girls from ST community, Benefits available for students in government and government-aided schools only, Payment made after Principal/Head of Institution recommendation and verification
docs_needed: Aadhaar card, ST Caste certificate issued by competent authority, Income certificate from Tahasildar (family income from all sources), Previous year marksheet (Class 8 for Class 9 admission, Class 10 for Class 11 admission), Admission letter or School enrollment certificate, Bank passbook with Aadhaar-linked account, Recent passport size photograph
step_guide: 1. Application is NOT submitted by students directly - Process handled through school Principal/Head Master, 2. Students must inform school Principal about their eligibility for the scheme, 3. Principal/Head of Institution logs into State Scholarship Portal at https://scholarship.odisha.gov.in/, 4. Principal fills online application on behalf of eligible ST students, 5. Required student details entered: Personal info, academic details, bank account, Aadhaar number, 6. Principal uploads all required documents digitally, 7. Principal validates and recommends the application online, 8. Application submitted by school authority before deadline, 9. ST & SC Development Department verifies applications at district level, 10. Eligible students receive ₹5,000 via DBT to their bank account, 11. Students can track status through scholarship portal using application number
selection: Only for Scheduled Tribe (ST) students who are bonafide residents of Odisha. Student must have completed Class 8 and newly admitted to Class 9 in same academic year, OR completed Class 10 and newly admitted to Class 11 in same academic year. This is a ONE-TIME incentive given at time of first admission only - cannot apply again in subsequent years. Family annual income from all sources must not exceed ₹2,50,000. Student must be enrolled in regular full-time courses in government or government-aided high schools and higher secondary schools within Odisha state. Private school students are NOT eligible. Students already receiving other ST scholarships like Post-Matric ST remain eligible for this scheme as it can be combined. Selection based on eligibility verification - all eligible ST students who meet criteria will receive scholarship (not merit-based competition). Application window opens twice yearly - applications invited during July-August and December-January cycles. School Principal must validate application - individual student applications not accepted.
renewal: No - One-time only
intro_seo: The Shahid Madho Singh Haath Kharcha Yojana provides one-time financial incentive of ₹5,000 to Scheduled Tribe students in Odisha joining Class 9 after completing Class 8 or joining Class 11 after completing Class 10 in government and government-aided schools with family income below ₹2.5 lakh annually to reduce dropout rates and encourage ST students to continue their secondary and higher secondary education without financial barriers.

title: Banishree Scholarship (Odisha)
level: School (1-5), School (6-12), Undergraduate (UG), Postgraduate (PG), Diploma/Polytechnic, ITI/ITC
amount_description: Primary (I-V): ₹4,800/year; Middle & High School (VI-X): ₹6,000/year; College (Plus-II & Plus-III): ₹7,200/year; PG/Technical/Vocational: ₹8,400/year. Additional allowances for transport/readers where applicable. Paid via DBT.
benefits: Monthly financial assistance ranging from ₹400 to ₹700 based on education level, Primary School (Class 1-5): ₹400 per month (₹4,800 annually), Middle and High School (Class 6-11): ₹500 per month (₹6,000 annually), College/University (+2 and above): ₹700 per month (₹8,400 annually), Students studying outside Odisha: ₹250 per month (if course unavailable in Odisha and not covered by Government of India scholarships), Additional Reader Allowance for Visually Impaired: ₹400 per month (requires institution certification of continuous reader employment), Additional Transport Allowance for Orthopedically Handicapped: ₹160 per month, Payment in two installments per academic year through Direct Benefit Transfer, Scholarship continues even if student fails a year as long as enrolled and attending regularly, Medical absence up to 3 months does not stop payment, Covers special schools registered under PWD Act 1995 not receiving government grant-in-aid, Supports distance education from recognized universities and government institutes, Includes technical vocational and professional training institutes, Over 23,600 students benefited in 2024-25, Scheme operational since April 1 2008, Recent increase in amounts announced December 21 2024
docs_needed: Aadhaar card, Disability Certificate issued by Medical Board (must show 40% or more disability), Last examination marksheet attested by Head of Institution or gazetted officer, Income Certificate issued by Tahasildar or competent authority (Not required for BPL families), Residence certificate or proof of Odisha residency, Bank passbook with account number and IFSC code (students above 18 must have individual account, minors need joint account with parent/guardian), Recent passport size photograph
step_guide: 1. Visit official SSEPD Portal at https://ssepd.odisha.gov.in/ or State Scholarship Portal at https://scholarship.odisha.gov.in/, 2. On homepage click on Banishree Scholarship option, 3. Click on New Application and then Proceed button, 4. Complete Aadhaar-based verification by entering Aadhaar number, 5. Fill application form with complete details: Name, Gender, Date of Birth, Type of Disability, Age, Police Station, District, Subdivision, Block, Village/Town, Citizenship status, Caste category, 6. Enter educational details: Institution name, Course, Class/Year, 7. Upload all required documents: Disability certificate, Income certificate, Last marksheet, Bank details, Photograph, 8. Enter bank account details: Account number, Account holder name, IFSC code, MICR code, 9. Review all entered information carefully, 10. Click Submit button to complete application, 11. selection: Based on verification of benchmark disability (≥ 40% certified by medical board) and annual family income ≤ ₹60,000.

intro_seo: The Banishree Scholarship provides monthly financial assistance ranging from ₹400 to ₹700 per month (₹4,800 to ₹8,400 annually) to students with disabilities in Odisha pursuing education from primary to postgraduate level in recognized institutions with family income below ₹60,000 per year including additional reader allowance for visually impaired students and transport allowance for orthopedically handicapped students to reduce dropout rates and promote inclusive education.

title: Post-Matric Scholarship for SC Students (Karnataka)
level: Post-Matric (Class 11 onwards including PUC, ITI, Diploma, UG, PG, Professional courses)
amount_description: Full tuition fee reimbursement and maintenance allowance ranging from ₹2,500 to ₹13,500 per year depending on the course group, disbursed directly via SSP.
benefits: Fee Reimbursement: Complete reimbursement of tuition fees, admission fees, and examination fees paid to the institution and Karnataka Examinations Authority (KEA). Maintenance Allowance: Rs 7,000 per year for day scholar students (not residing in government hostels). Rs 13,500 per year for hosteller students (residing in government hostels). Hostel Fee Support: Reimbursement of hostel fees for students in both government and private hostels (subject to verification). Counselling Support: 2/3rd of counselling fee reimbursement for students in counselling-based admission courses. Direct Benefit Transfer: All scholarship amounts credited directly to student's Aadhaar-linked bank account through DBT. Additional Benefits: 30% quota reserved for female students. Freeship Card available for download after completing Aadhaar e-KYC. No need to visit multiple offices - fully online process.
docs_needed: SC Caste Certificate, Income Certificate (annual family income <= Rs 2.5 lakh), Previous year mark sheets (minimum 50% marks), Admission receipt/fee receipt, Aadhaar card, Bank account passbook (Aadhaar-linked), Domicile certificate/proof of Karnataka residency, Academic records from UUCMS portal or university, NSP One Time Registration (OTR) ID - MANDATORY, For hostellers: Registration in State Hostel Portal (SHP) or SSP-HMIS portal
step_guide: Step 1: Complete NSP One Time Registration (MANDATORY FIRST STEP) - Visit scholarships.gov.in (National Scholarship Portal). Click 'One Time Registration (OTR)'. Complete Aadhaar-based eKYC and Face Authentication using NSP OTR App. Receive your OTR Number - REQUIRED for SSP application. Step 2: Create SSP Account - Visit https://ssp.postmatric.karnataka.gov.in. Click 'Create New Account'. Enter Aadhaar number, name matching Aadhaar, mobile number, email ID. Verify OTP sent to mobile. Link Aadhaar with bank account (mandatory for DBT). Step 3: Fill Application Form - Log in to SSP portal with credentials. Click 'Apply for Post-Matric Scholarship'. Select 'Social Welfare Department - SC Category'. Enter NSP OTR ID (from Step 1). Fill personal details (name, date of birth, gender, contact details). Enter academic information (course, institution, year, previous exam marks). Provide family details (father/mother name, occupation, annual income). Enter bank account details (Aadhaar-linked account mandatory). Answer domicile question (Are you resident of Karnataka? Yes). For hostellers: Select 'Hosteller' ONLY if in government-run hostel (private hostel/PG = day scholar). Step 4: Upload Documents - Upload clear scanned copies of all required documents (see 'Documents Needed' section). Ensure file format and size requirements are met. Documents must be legible and complete. Step 5: Submit Application - Preview filled application carefully. Check all details for accuracy. Tick declaration checkbox confirming information is true. Click 'Final Submit'. Download and save acknowledgment slip. Note the Application Reference Number. Step 6: Institutional Verification (E-Attestation) - Submit printed acknowledgment slip to your college/school office. College principal/head will verify your application online. E-attestation officer will verify uploaded documents. Track this process in your SSP login dashboard. Step 7: Track Application Status - Log in to SSP portal regularly. Check application status (Submitted/Under Verification/Approved/Rejected). Monitor for any requests for document clarification. Check payment status once approved.

title: Post-Matric Scholarship for Minorities (Karnataka)
level: Post-Matric (Class 11 onwards including PUC, ITI, Diploma, General Degree, Professional/Technical Courses, UG, PG). NOT applicable for correspondence/distance learning or certificate/training programs.
caste: Minority Communities: Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Buddhist, Jain, Parsi (Zoroastrian)
amount_description: Consists of full fee reimbursement for tuition/admission fees and a maintenance allowance ranging from ₹3,500 to ₹20,000 per year based on study level and hosteller status. Disbursed directly to Aadhaar-seeded, NPCI-mapped bank accounts via DBT.
benefits: Fee Reimbursement: Complete reimbursement of tuition fees and admission fees. Full payment of fees paid to recognized government or private institutions. Maintenance Allowance - Varies by Level: Class XI-XII/PUC: Rs 3,500-5,000/year. Diploma/ITI/Polytechnic: Rs 5,000-7,000/year. Undergraduate (UG): Rs 7,000-12,000/year. Postgraduate (PG)/Professional: Rs 10,000-20,000/year. Hosteller vs Day Scholar: Higher rates for hosteller students. Lower rates for day scholar students. Duration: Benefits provided for 10 months in an academic year (not 12 months). PMS vs MCM Distinction: PMS (Post-Matric Scholarship): For PUC, general degree, diploma, ITI courses. Income limit Rs 2 lakh. MCM (Merit-Cum-Means): For professional/technical undergraduate and postgraduate courses. Income limit Rs 2.5 lakh. Higher maintenance allowance rates. Direct Benefit Transfer: All amounts credited to student's Aadhaar-linked bank account through DBT with NPCI mapping. Family Limit: Maximum two children per family eligible across all Ministry of Minority Affairs scholarships. NOT Eligible For: Correspondence or distance learning courses. Certificate or training programs. Students receiving other central government-funded scholarships.
docs_needed: Minority Community Certificate (Muslim/Christian/Sikh/Buddhist/Jain/Parsi), Income Certificate (PMS: <= Rs 2 lakh OR MCM: <= Rs 2.5 lakh), Previous year/semester mark sheets (>= 50% marks), Admission receipt/fee receipt, Aadhaar card, Bank account passbook (Aadhaar-linked with NPCI mapping), Domicile certificate (Karnataka residency proof), Identity card issued by institution, SSLC and PUC mark sheets, All semester/year mark sheets and degree certificates (if applicable), Copy of competitive exam result/scorecard (if applicable for admission), NSP One Time Registration (OTR) Number - MANDATORY, For e-attestation: Original documents to be shown to e-attestation officer
deadline: September 30, 2025 (tentative - some sources indicate November 30, 2025 or extended deadlines). VERIFY on official NSP and SSP portals for exact date.
step_guide: Step 1: NSP One Time Registration (MANDATORY FIRST PORTAL) - Visit https://scholarships.gov.in (National Scholarship Portal). Click 'One Time Registration (OTR)'. Download NSP OTR Mobile App for Face Authentication. Complete Aadhaar-based eKYC using Face Authentication in app. Receive your OTR Number - this is CRITICAL for both NSP and SSP applications. Step 2: Apply on NSP (FIRST APPLICATION) - Log in to NSP with OTR credentials. Select 'Post-Matric Scholarship for Minorities' scheme. Select Karnataka as your state. Choose PMS (general courses, income <= Rs 2L) OR MCM (professional courses, income <= Rs 2.5L). Fill application form with personal, academic, family, bank details. Upload all required documents on NSP portal. Submit NSP application. Note your NSP Application ID - REQUIRED for SSP application. Step 3: Apply on SSP (SECOND APPLICATION) - Visit https://ssp.postmatric.karnataka.gov.in. Create account or log in. Click 'Apply for Post-Matric Scholarship'. Select 'Minority Welfare Department'. Enter your NSP Application ID from Step 2 (MANDATORY field). Select minority community (Muslim/Christian/Sikh/Buddhist/Jain/Parsi). Fill SSP application form. Upload documents again on SSP (may require re-upload). Confirm domicile status (Karnataka resident? Yes). Step 4: E-Attestation (SSP Specific) - Visit SSP e-attestation portal separately. Upload documents for e-attestation. Visit your designated e-attestation officer IN PERSON with ORIGINAL documents. Officer will verify and e-attest your documents online. selection: Selection is based on category eligibility (belonging to Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Buddhist, Jain, or Parsi community), securing at least 50% in the previous final exam, and family annual income not exceeding ₹2,00,000.

intro_seo: The Post-Matric Scholarship for Minorities in Karnataka provides financial assistance to students from minority communities (Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Buddhist, Jain, Parsi) pursuing education after Class 10. This scholarship has two components: PMS (Post-Matric Scholarship) for general courses with income limit Rs 2 lakh, and MCM (Merit-Cum-Means) for professional/technical courses with income limit Rs 2.5 lakh. Students must apply through BOTH National Scholarship Portal (NSP) first, then State Scholarship Portal (SSP). Karnataka domicile students studying outside the state are also eligible.

title: Post-Matric Scholarship for EWS/General Category (Karnataka)

title: Post-Matric Scholarship for Persons with Disabilities - UDID (Karnataka)
level: Post-Matric (Class 11 onwards including PUC, ITI, Diploma, UG, PG, Professional courses)
caste: All categories (SC/ST/OBC/Minority/General) - must possess valid Unique Disability ID (UDID) card issued by Department for Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities, Government of India.
benefits: Fee Reimbursement: Tuition fee coverage - amount varies by course type. Admission fee reimbursement. Examination fee support where applicable. Maintenance Allowance: Estimated Rs 2,500 to Rs 13,500 per year (exact amount not clearly documented). Varies based on: Disability type and severity. Disability percentage (fetched from UDID database). Course level (PUC vs UG vs PG vs Professional). Student type (day scholar vs hosteller - if hosteller provision exists). UDID System Integration: Disability data auto-populated from central database when UDID number entered. Eliminates need for separate disability verification in most cases. Disability percentage automatically fetched. Disability type automatically populated. Cross-Caste Support: Available to disabled students across ALL caste categories: SC students with disability can apply. ST students with disability can apply. OBC students with disability can apply. Minority students with disability can apply. General category students with disability can apply. Direct Benefit Transfer: All amounts credited to student's Aadhaar-linked bank account. Extended Deadline: Longer application window (December 31) compared to other categories, recognizing additional documentation needs for disabled students.
docs_needed: UDID (Unique Disability ID) Card - MANDATORY, Disability Certificate (if separate from UDID), Previous year mark sheets (minimum 50% marks), Income Certificate (family income <= Rs 2.5 lakh estimated), Admission receipt/fee receipt, Aadhaar card, Bank account passbook (Aadhaar-linked), Domicile certificate/Karnataka residency proof, Caste certificate (if applicable for SC/ST/OBC/Minority), Academic records from institution, Medical certificate specifying disability type and percentage (may auto-populate from UDID database)
step_guide: Step 1: Obtain UDID Card (If Not Already Have) - Visit https://www.swavlambancard.gov.in (National UDID Portal). Click 'Enrolment for UDID Card'. Fill enrollment form with personal details and disability information. Upload disability certificate and medical documents. Submit for verification. Receive UDID number and card (may take time for processing). IMPORTANT: Complete this BEFORE SSP scholarship application if you don't have UDID. Step 2: Create SSP Account - Visit https://ssp.postmatric.karnataka.gov.in. Click 'Create New Account'. Enter Aadhaar number, name, mobile, email. Verify OTP. Link Aadhaar with bank account (mandatory for DBT). Step 3: Fill Application Form - Log in to SSP portal. Click 'Apply for Post-Matric Scholarship'. Select 'Department for Empowerment of Differently Abled and Senior Citizens' OR 'Disability Welfare Department'. Fill personal details (name, DOB, gender, contact). Answer domicile question: 'Are you a resident of Karnataka?' - Select YES. Step 4: Enter UDID Information (CRITICAL STEP) - When prompted for disability information, select 'Yes' for 'Do you have disability?'. Enter your UDID number in the designated field. Click 'Get Data' or 'Fetch Details' button. System will automatically populate: Disability type (locomotor/visual/hearing/intellectual/etc.). Disability percentage (e.g., 40%, 60%, 80%). This data comes directly from central UDID database - verify it appears correctly. Step 5: Complete Application - Fill academic information (course, institution, previous exam marks >= 50%). Enter family details (parent names, occupation, annual income). Enter bank account details (Aadhaar-linked account). Upload required documents (see 'Documents Needed' section). IMPORTANT: For hosteller status, select 'Hosteller' ONLY if living in government-run hostel. Private hostel/PG accommodation = Day Scholar. Step 6: Submit Application - Preview application carefully. Verify UDID data auto-populated correctly. Verify all disability information is accurate. Check declaration checkbox. Click 'Final Submit'. Download acknowledgment slip and note Application Reference Number. Step 7: Institutional Verification - Submit acknowledgment slip to your college/school office. Submit UDID card copy and other required documents to institution. College principal/head will verify application online. Track verification status in your SSP login. Step 8: Track Application Status - Log in to SSP portal regularly. Check application status (Submitted/Under Verification/Approved/Rejected). If status shows 'Invalid Document', address issues immediately before deadline. Monitor payment status once approved.
selection: Selection based on: UDID Validation: System verifies UDID number with central database (swavlambancard.gov.in). UDID must be active and valid. Disability type and percentage cross-checked automatically. Students without valid UDID are ineligible. Eligibility Verification: Karnataka domicile status verified through certificates. Income verification (limit not clearly specified - likely <= Rs 2.5 lakh). Caste verification if applicable (SC/ST/OBC/Minority with disability). Academic Performance: Minimum 50% marks in previous examination mandatory. Academic progression and enrollment verified. Institutional Validation: Student enrollment in recognized Karnataka institution verified. College principal/head approval required. Academic records cross-checked. Document Verification: All uploaded documents validated. Aadhaar and bank account seeding verified. UDID card and disability certificate authenticity checked. Priority/Consideration: Differently-abled students across all caste categories eligible. Extended deadline (Dec 31) allows more time for disabled students to complete applications. Processing: Applications processed by Department for Empowerment of Differently Abled and Senior Citizens, Government of Karnataka. Budget allocation for disability welfare determines approvals. Disbursement: Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) to student's Aadhaar-linked bank account. Payment timeline typically February-July after final approval.
renewal: Yes - renewable annually. Renewal conditions: Maintain minimum 50% marks each year. Continue meeting income eligibility. UDID card must remain valid. Fresh application required each academic year through SSP portal.
helpline: SSP Helpline: 1902. Email: [email protected]. Department for Empowerment of Differently Abled and Senior Citizens, Government of Karnataka (for disability-specific queries). UDID Helpline: Check https://www.swavlambancard.gov.in for UDID card related queries.
intro_seo: The Post-Matric Scholarship for Persons with Disabilities in Karnataka provides financial assistance to differently-abled students pursuing education after Class 10. Administered by the Department for the Empowerment of Differently Abled and Senior Citizens through the State Scholarship Portal (SSP), this scholarship requires students to possess a valid UDID (Unique Disability ID) card. The system automatically fetches disability type and percentage from the central UDID database. Students must be permanent Karnataka residents with minimum 50% marks in previous exam.

title: Vidyasiri Food and Accommodation Scholarship for OBC Students (Karnataka)
level: Post-Matric (Classes 11/PUC onwards including Diploma, ITI, General Degree, Professional courses, UG, PG)
caste: Other Backward Classes (OBC) - Category 1, Category 2A, Category 3A, Category 3B, Nomadic Tribes (NT), Semi-Nomadic Tribes (SNT). Caste must be in OBC list notified by Government of India or State Government.
gender: All. Special provision: Only two male members from same family eligible; no restriction for female students (unlimited female students from same family can apply).
amount_description: Provides a monthly stipend of ₹1,500 for 10 months (totaling ₹15,000 per annum) to cover food and accommodation expenses for students who do not get admission to government hostels. Disbursed via Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) into the student's Aadhaar-linked bank account.
benefits: Monthly Stipend: Rs 1,500 per month credited directly to student's Aadhaar-linked bank account. Duration: 10 months per academic year (not 12 months). Total Annual Amount: Rs 15,000 per year. Purpose: Covers food and accommodation expenses for students NOT in government hostels. Payment Method: Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) through core banking system. Merit-Cum-Means Distribution: Scholarship distributed on merit-cum-means basis within reserved categories. Gender Support: No limit on number of female students from same family. Male Limit: Only two male members per family eligible. CRITICAL RESTRICTION: Students CANNOT avail this scholarship if they: (i) Have admission in government-funded hostel, (ii) Are receiving boarding and lodging assistance, (iii) Are receiving any other post-matric scholarship. Only ONE benefit allowed - must choose between Vidyasiri Food & Accommodation OR Post-Matric OBC Scholarship OR Government Hostel facility.
docs_needed: OBC Caste Certificate (must be in GoI/State notified list), Income Certificate (Category 1: <= Rs 2.5L OR Category 2A/3A/3B: <= Rs 1L), Previous year mark sheets (meeting minimum % by category), 75% attendance certificate, Proof of residence (must be >= 5 km away from college in rural areas OR in different city/town from college location), Aadhaar card, Bank account passbook with core banking system (Aadhaar-linked for DBT), Domicile certificate/Indian citizenship proof, Academic records, Transfer Certificate (TC) from school/college, Declaration: NOT admitted to any departmental or government-funded hostel, Declaration: NOT receiving other post-matric scholarship or hostel facility
deadline: December 12, 2025 (verify on official portal - multiple sources show Oct 31 to Dec 12, 2025)
step_guide: Step 1: Verify Eligibility - Confirm OBC category, family income within limit, NOT in government hostel, residence >= 5 km from college (rural) OR studying in different city/town, academic marks meet requirement, 75% attendance maintained. Step 2: Create SSP Account at https://ssp.postmatric.karnataka.gov.in with Aadhaar. Step 3: Fill Application - Select Backward Classes Welfare Department, choose Vidyasiri Food and Accommodation Scholarship, fill details, declare no government hostel admission. Step 4: Upload Documents - OBC certificate, income certificate, mark sheets, attendance certificate, residence proof, Aadhaar, bank passbook. Step 5: Submit and download acknowledgment. Step 6: Submit hard copy to college. Step 7: Track status on portal.
selection: Selection is based on caste category (OBC), academic performance (minimum 55% for Category 1, 65% for Category 2A/3A/3B in previous qualifying exam), distance from college (minimum 5 km from native place), and annual family income limits (₹2.5 lakh for Category 1, ₹1 lakh for Categories 2A, 3A, 3B).
renewal: Renewals must be submitted annually on the SSP portal. The student must have passed the previous academic year without backlogs, maintain regular attendance, and not be residing in any government-run hostel.
intro_seo: The Vidyasiri Food and Accommodation Scholarship for OBC Students in Karnataka provides financial assistance to Other Backward Classes students pursuing post-matric education who are NOT residing in government hostels. Offered by the Department of Backward Classes Welfare through the State Scholarship Portal (SSP), this scholarship provides Rs 15,000 per year (Rs 1,500/month for 10 months) to cover food and accommodation expenses. Students must be permanent Karnataka residents living at least 5 km away from their college (rural areas) or studying in a different city/town, with family income up to Rs 1-2.5 lakh depending on OBC sub-category.

title: Merit-Cum-Means (MCM) Scholarship for Minorities - Professional and Technical Courses (Karnataka)
provider_type: Central Government (Ministry of Minority Affairs) + State Government (Karnataka Directorate of Minorities)
level: Post-Matric Professional/Technical: UG and PG professional/technical courses ONLY. Admission must be through competitive examination.
caste: Minority Communities: Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Buddhist, Jain, Parsi (Zoroastrian)
amount_description: Provides a maintenance allowance of ₹20,000 per annum (or full course fee reimbursement for students admitted to premier listed institutions). The disbursement is processed through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) directly into the student's Aadhaar-seeded, NPCI-mapped bank account.
benefits: Maintenance Allowance: Rs 20,000 per annum. Premier Institutes (85 listed): Full course fee reimbursement + Rs 20,000 maintenance (IITs, NITs, IIMs). Other Institutions: Rs 20,000 maintenance + partial fee support. Direct Benefit Transfer to Aadhaar-linked account. Central + State Support. Family Limit: Max 2 children per family across ALL MoMA scholarships. Higher Income Limit: Rs 2.5 lakh (vs Rs 2 lakh for PMS). Professional Course Focus for employability. NOT for correspondence/distance learning, certificate programs, general degrees, or students receiving other central govt scholarships.
docs_needed: Minority Community Certificate, Income Certificate from Tahsildar (<= Rs 2.5 lakh), Previous year mark sheet (>= 50% marks), Admission certificate from institute, Tuition fee receipt, Proof of admission through competitive exam, Aadhaar card, Bank account passbook (Aadhaar-linked), Domicile certificate, Identity card from institution, NSP One Time Registration (OTR) Number - MANDATORY
deadline: September 30, 2025 (tentative - verify on NSP and SSP portals)
step_guide: Step 1: Verify Eligibility - Minority community, professional/technical course, competitive exam admission, income <= Rs 2.5L, >= 50% marks, max 2 siblings in MoMA schemes. Step 2: NSP OTR Registration (MANDATORY FIRST) - Visit scholarships.gov.in, complete One Time Registration with Aadhaar Face Authentication via NSP OTR App, receive OTR Number. Step 3: Apply on NSP (PRIMARY) - Login with OTR, select MCM for Minorities scheme, select Karnataka, fill form, upload documents, submit, note NSP Application ID. Step 4: Apply on Karnataka SSP (SECONDARY) - Visit ssp.postmatric.karnataka.gov.in, select Minority Welfare MCM, enter NSP Application ID (mandatory), fill SSP form, upload documents, complete e-attestation if required. Step 5: Submit hard copies to college if required. Step 6: Track on NSP, SSP, and PFMS (pfms.nic.in) portals.
selection: Selection is based on merit (securing minimum 50% marks in the previous qualifying exam and admission to professional/technical courses) and means (annual family income must not exceed ₹2.5 lakh).
renewal: Renewals are submitted on the SSP portal using existing credentials. The candidate must have passed the previous academic year's examinations without backlogs, maintain regular attendance, and obtain institutional verification.
intro_seo: The Merit-Cum-Means Scholarship for Minorities (MCM) in Karnataka provides financial assistance to minority community students pursuing professional and technical courses at UG and PG levels. Administered by Ministry of Minority Affairs through NSP and Karnataka Directorate of Minorities through SSP, this scholarship offers Rs 20,000 per year. Students admitted to 85 premier institutes (IITs, NITs, IIMs) receive full course fee reimbursement plus maintenance. title: Pre-Matric SC Students (Karnataka)
amount_description: Financial assistance for education. Historically, classes 1-5 get ₹1,000 (boys) / ₹1,100 (girls), and classes 6-8 get ₹1,150 (boys) / ₹1,250 (girls). Class 9-10 rates vary. Paid via Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) to Aadhaar-seeded bank account.
benefits: The scholarship provides full tuition fee coverage plus maintenance allowance based on day scholar or hosteller status. State-funded scheme for Classes 1-8 has higher income limit (₹6L) while centrally-funded scheme for Classes 9-10 has stricter limit (₹2.5L). Day scholars receive ₹1,000-₹3,000 annually for educational expenses, while hostellers get ₹7,000 for accommodation and meals. Freeship Card available after completing e-KYC provides additional fee waivers. Scholarship amount directly credited to student's Aadhaar-linked bank account via DBT. Auto-renewal available for continuing students who received scholarship in previous year.
docs_needed: (1) SATS ID (Student Achievement Track System ID), (2) Aadhaar card linked to bank account, (3) SC caste certificate issued by Revenue Department, (4) Income certificate from Revenue Department, (5) Previous year marks card (not required for Class 1), (6) Bank account passbook (Aadhaar-seeded and NPCI-mapped), (7) Recent passport-size photograph, (8) School bonafide certificate, (9) Freeship Card (downloadable after e-KYC completion)
deadline: January 15, 2026 (tentative for 2025-26 cycle; verify on portal before applying)
step_guide: (1) Obtain SATS ID from school or retrieve from https://sts.karnataka.gov.in/KMSID using student name, DOB, and school details; (2) Visit https://ssp.karnataka.gov.in and click 'Create Account' under Pre-Matric section; (3) Enter SATS ID, Aadhaar number, mobile number, and email to register; (4) Set password and complete account creation; (5) Login with SSP ID and password; (6) Complete e-KYC by entering Aadhaar number and OTP verification; (7) Fill application form with personal details, academic information, and category details; (8) Upload all required documents in specified format (PDF/JPG, max 200KB each); (9) Verify all details carefully as corrections have limited window; (10) Submit application and download acknowledgment receipt; (11) Download Freeship Card after e-KYC approval; (12) Track application status through 'Track Student Scholarship Status' option using SATS ID
selection: Based on family income limits (Classes 1-8: ≤ ₹6 Lakh; Classes 9-10: ≤ ₹2.5 Lakh) and category/caste verification. Verification-based direct benefit for eligible applicants.
renewal: Requires promotion to the next class and school verification. Auto-renewal is facilitated on the SSP portal for existing eligible students.

title: Pudhumai Penn Scheme (Tamil Nadu)
provider: Social Welfare and Women Empowerment Department, Government of Tamil Nadu
amount_description: Provides a monthly financial assistance of ₹1,000 disbursed directly to the girl student's bank account for the duration of her higher education course.
benefits:
* Monthly incentive of ₹1,000 (total ₹12,000/year) sent via DBT.
* Financial support covers the entire duration of the UG degree/diploma/ITI course.
docs_needed:
* School Transfer Certificate (TC) proving study from Classes 6 to 12 in Govt schools
* Aadhaar Card
* Bank Account Passbook (Aadhaar-linked)
* College Admission Card / Bonafide Certificate
* Marksheets
step_guide:
### Step-by-Step Application Guide
1. **Collect School Proofs:** Gather your school transfer certificate (TC) or study certificates proving you studied in government schools from Classes 6 to 12.
2. **College Help Desk:** The primary application route is through your college. Colleges host a dedicated registration drive on the Penkalvi/UMIS portal.
3. **Submit Details to Nodal Officer:** Submit your Aadhaar card copy, bank details, and school certificates to your college's designated nodal officer.
4. **Portal Upload:** The college will upload your documents and verify your active enrollment.
5. **DBT Activation:** Ensure your bank account is active and seeded with Aadhaar to receive the monthly ₹1,000 direct benefit transfers.
selection: Exclusively for female students who completed their schooling (Class 6 to 12) in Tamil Nadu Government schools and are pursuing regular higher education courses.
renewal: Automatically renewed monthly based on the college verifying the student's active enrollment and attendance through the UMIS portal.

step_guide:
### Step-by-Step Application Guide:
1. **Visit the official portal:** Go to the Chief Minister's Relief Fund (CMRF) website at [cmrf.wb.gov.in](https://cmrf.wb.gov.in).
2. **Register Online:** Click on "Apply for Financial Assistance for Education" and register with your mobile number.
3. **Fill the Form:** Log in and fill in all personal, academic, and banking details.
4. **Attestation & Recommendation:** Obtain a recommendation letter from your local MLA/MP. Scan and upload it along with other documents.
5. **Upload Documents:** Upload scanned copies of your marksheet, admission receipt, income certificate, and bank passbook.
6. **Submit:** Double-check your details and submit the application online. Keep a printout of the reference number for tracking.

title: Oasis Pre-Matric Scholarship for SC Students (West Bengal)
provider: Backward Classes Welfare Department, Government of West Bengal
amount_description: Maintenance allowance: ₹150/month for day scholars, ₹350/month for hostellers (paid for 10 months). Ad-hoc grant: ₹750/year for day scholars, ₹1,000/year for hostellers.
benefits: Maintenance allowance of ₹1,500 to ₹3,500 per annum, plus an annual ad-hoc grant of ₹750 (day scholars) or ₹1,000 (hostellers) to cover school expenses and tuition fees.
docs_needed:
Aadhaar Card
Caste Certificate issued by WB Government
Previous Class Marksheet
Income Certificate
Bank Passbook showing IFSC and Account Number
Passport-size Photograph
School Bonafide Certificate
step_guide:
### Step-by-Step Application Guide:
1. **Visit Oasis Portal:** Go to the official OASIS website at [oasis.gov.in](https://oasis.gov.in).
2. **Student Registration:** Click on the "Student's Registration" link. Select the district where your school is located.
3. **Verify Caste Certificate:** Enter your computerised Caste Certificate number and issue date, then submit.
4. **Fill Basic Details:** Enter your school name, class, mobile number, email, and bank account details. Submit to generate your Application ID and Password.
5. **Login and Apply:** Log in using your credentials. Click "Apply Now" and fill out the detailed form (academic info, parent income details).
6. **Bank Verification:** Enter your bank account details (IFSC, Account Number) carefully. Ensure Aadhaar is linked.
7. **Verify & Lock:** Double-check all entered information. Click "Verify and Lock Application". Note: No changes can be made after locking.
8. **Print & Submit:** Download the PDF application form. Print it, attach the required documents (income cert, marksheet, caste cert, bank proof), and submit it to your school Principal for verification.
selection: Based on satisfying the eligibility criteria: West Bengal domicile, valid SC caste certificate, enrollment in Class 9 or 10, and annual family income below ₹2.5 Lakh.
renewal: Must reapply online each academic year through the Oasis portal using the renewal login option.
intro_seo: Apply for the Oasis Pre-Matric Scholarship for SC Students in West Bengal. Check official eligibility criteria, annual family income limits, required documents list, monthly stipends, and online step-by-step application guide for Class 9 and 10 SC day scholars and hostellers on oasis.gov.in.

title: Kanyashree Prakalpa Scheme (K1 & K2) (West Bengal)
provider: Department of Women and Child Development and Social Welfare, Government of West Bengal
amount_description: K1 (Annual Stipend): ₹1,000/year for unmarried girls aged 13-18 (Class 8-12). K2 (One-Time Grant): ₹25,000 paid upon turning 18, provided the student remains unmarried and is enrolled in higher education or training.
benefits: Annual stipend of ₹1,000 for younger students (K1) and a major one-time developmental grant of ₹25,000 (K2) to promote girl child education, prevent early marriage, and support higher studies or vocational training.
age_limit: 13-18 years for K1; 18 years for K2
docs_needed:
Aadhaar Card

step_guide:
### Step-by-Step Application Guide:
1. **Obtain Form:** Collect the Kanyashree Application Form (Form K1 for annual scholarship, Form K2 for one-time grant) free of cost from your school or college.
2. **Fill Details:** Complete the form carefully with personal details, bank account information, and institutional details.
3. **Open Bank Account:** Ensure you have a bank account in your name. WBMDFC/School authorities often help arrange student bank accounts.
4. **Sign Declarations:** The student must sign the unmarried declaration. The parent/guardian must also sign the declaration of marital status and family income.
5. **Attach Documents:** Gather copies of your Aadhaar, birth certificate, bank passbook, previous marksheet, and passport photo.
6. **Submit to School:** Submit the filled form with attached documents directly to your school/college office.
7. **School Portal Upload:** The institution authorities will verify your documents and upload the data to the official Kanyashree Portal.
8. **Receive Acknowledgment:** You will receive a system-generated acknowledgment slip with your Kanyashree ID for tracking your status online.
selection: Direct benefit transfer upon satisfying all eligibility criteria (unmarried female, domicile of West Bengal, age limits, regular educational enrollment, and family income below ₹1.2 Lakh). The income limit is waived for orphans, JJ Home inmates, and students with >40% disability.
renewal: K1 is renewed annually through the school portal. K2 is a one-time grant upon turning 18 and requires a separate application.
